children.name: Ibidorhyncha
children.description: A monotypic genus instantly recognisable by its long, downward-curved red bill, which it uses to probe under stones in shallow mountain streams. The Ibisbill is a highly specialised shorebird of the high-altitude rivers of Central Asia, straddling the ecological niche between an ibis and a plover.

children.name: Ibidorhyncha struthersii
children.description: A distinctive shorebird of gravel-bed rivers in the mountains of Central Asia, from the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau to the Tien Shan. Its long, decurved bill allows it to extract insect larvae and crustaceans from beneath pebbles in fast-flowing water. It is well-camouflaged against the grey stones of its riverbed habitat.
